steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The decisive clue is the phrase “certain drawbacks made themselves very manifest” — the reflexive verb “made themselves” signals that the drawbacks revealed or showed themselves, becoming plainly visible to Katharine as time passed.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Possible’: “Possible” would mean the drawbacks merely might exist, but the passage says they actively revealed themselves — they are clearly present, not just potential. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Evident’: “Evident” means clearly visible or apparent, which matches exactly how drawbacks “make themselves manifest” — they become unmistakably obvious. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Complex’: “Complex” describes difficulty or intricacy, not visibility; nothing in the passage suggests Katharine finds the drawbacks hard to understand rather than simply apparent. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Particular’: “Particular” would mean specific or distinctive, which does not capture the idea that the drawbacks have become clearly perceptible over time. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
